prompt: An authentic 17th-century European copperplate engraving depicting a mountainous landscape as a metaphor for iterative descent. A narrow winding trail is carved into the terrain, descending step-by-step from a steep summit into a deep valley. The descending path includes clearly defined step-like segments or terraces, each subtly marked as distinct stages of progression. Along the path, small engraved markers, stones, or banners indicate progression, rendered as hand-drawn cartographic annotations rather than mathematical symbols. Direction is shown through subtle etched marks and natural flow of the terrain, not abstract arrows. No modern mathematical notation or coordinate axes. All elements are physically embedded into the landscape, sharing the same engraved linework, cross-hatching, and stippling. Irregular, hand-crafted line quality, slight plate imperfections, uneven ink distribution, aged paper texture. Sepia monochrome. Composition resembles early natural philosophy and topographical atlas engravings.
tradition_name: Scientific copperplate engraving (European topographical and natural philosophy illustration)
tradition_approach: Fine cross-hatching, engraved linework, cartographic annotations, and physically embedded elements to replicate authentic copperplate engraving grammar and integrate the concept into the landscape.
